search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae ear pain otitis telehealth guideline PDF
candidate_sources_rejected: DHA common-cold guideline as an ear-pain source
otitis-media-with-effusion guidance as acute ear-pain coverage
rejection_reasons: The common-cold document only mentions otitis as a complication.
Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
OME guidance excludes acute otitis media.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for diffuse AOE age 2 years or older, paediatric acute otitis media, and selected adult hearing-related ear symptoms; not exact for all causes of otalgia.
setting_applicability: Partial: US specialty, UK health-service, and adult audiology guidance versus UAE general ENT documentation.
UAE_applicability: No exact UAE ear-pain guideline was identified; all mapped international guidance requires UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: The current AAO-HNSF AOE update, NICE NG91, and NICE NG98 were opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: The 2014 AOE update, NG91, and NG98 remain current official guidance identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped AOE, water-exposure, itching, fullness, hearing-change, external-ear tenderness, and host-factor fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports swimmer’s-ear terminology, water exposure, ear-canal inflammation, itching, fullness, hearing change, external-ear pain or tenderness, diabetes, immunocompromise, and tympanic-membrane context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-documented ear care, pain or symptom management, reassessment, and questions in diffuse AOE.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports distinguishing AOE from other otalgia or otorrhoea, pain assessment, clinician-selected topical management, administration discussion, and reassessment after non-response.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped paediatric AOM, fever, URTI, ear-pain, and reassessment fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines the pediatric acute-otitis-media population, usual duration, reassessment triggers, otorrhoea context, and serious-illness review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped mastoiditis and severe systemic-illness concern prompts in children.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires review for rapid worsening or systemic illness and identifies severe infection and acute complications including mastoiditis for hospital assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Reviewed for adult sudden or rapidly worsening hearing-loss escalation context; no additional generic ear-pain items were mapped from this section.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult otalgia, discharge, hearing change, vertigo, ear findings, and referral fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Reviewed for adult audiology assessment boundaries; no additional legacy ear-pain items were mapped beyond the more specific referral section.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

unresolved_source_gaps: The combined AOM or OE diagnosis label, bilateral generic ear pain, facial weakness without hearing loss, severe headache, neck, oropharyngeal, tonsillar, nasal, sinus, temperature, fixed one-week timing, and medication content remain unsupported or population limited.
No UAE-specific exact source was found.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
